ddls:
CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the post */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the post */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who created the post */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the post was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the post was last updated */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Status of the post (e.g., Draft, Published) */,
  "publish_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for scheduled publishing */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content (e.g., article, image, video) */,
  "excerpt" TEXT /* Short summary or excerpt of the post */,
  "featured_image_id" INTEGER /* ID of the featured image */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of views for the post */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the post */,
  "share_count" INTEGER /* Number of shares for the post */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_featured_image_id FOREIGN KEY ("featured_image_id") REFERENCES media ("media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES post_statuses ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post to which the comment is associated */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who made the comment */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the comment */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was last updated */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Status of the comment (e.g., Pending Approval, Approved) */,
  "parent_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent comment (for nested comments) */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the comment */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_parent_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES comment_statuses ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the CMS (e.g., editor, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "profile_picture_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's profile picture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_profile_picture_id FOREIGN KEY ("profile_picture_id") REFERENCES media ("media_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_statuses" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post status */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Draft, Published) */,
  "code" INTEGER /* Code representing the status */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of status category (e.g., PostStatus) */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Position for sorting statuses */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the status */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the status is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comment_statuses" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment status */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Pending Approval, Approved) */,
  "code" INTEGER /* Code representing the status */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of status category (e.g., CommentStatus) */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Position for sorting statuses */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the status */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the status is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the tag was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the tag */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the tag is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "post_tags" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated post */,
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id", "tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics record */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post associated with the analytics */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the post */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the post */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares for the post */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analytics record was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "media" (
  "media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media record */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post to which the media is associated */,
  "media_type" TEXT /* Type of media (e.g., image, video) */,
  "media_url" TEXT /* URL of the media file */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the media was created */,
  "alt_text" TEXT /* Alternative text for accessibility */,
  "caption" TEXT /* Caption for the media */,
  "is_featured" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the media is featured */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_media_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., author, editor, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the role is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
